devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच – । अत्रानुवर्ण्यतेऽभीक्ष्णं विश्वात्मा भगवान् हरिः । यस्य प्रसादजो ब्रह्मा रुद्रः क्रोधसमुद्भवः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca – atrānuvarṇyate yasya prasādajo brahmā rudraḥ krodhasamudbhavaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(This Bhāgavata Mahāpurāṇa inspires fear-less-ness even from death as) herein every now and then is extolled the Almighty Lord Hari, the Soul and controller of the universe, of whose gracious joy (a trait of rajas) was born god Brahmā, the creator of the world and of whose anger was created Ṛuḍra, the destroyer of the world (and both are hence subordinate to and dependent on him).

devanagari: त्वं तु राजन् मरिष्येति पशुबुद्धिमिमां जहि । न जातः प्रागभूतोऽद्य देहवत्त्वं न नङ्क्ष्यसि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ tu rājan mariṣyeti paśubuddhimimāṃ jahi na jātaḥ prāgabhūto

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: O King! Please do give up this brute-like (foolish) notion (identifying the Soul with one’s physical body) that you will die. Unlike (your) body (which did not exist before the moment of its birth and hence shall naturally perish), you i.e. your soul did pre-exist (your physical body), is now in existence and shall never die (irrespective of what happens to your body).

devanagari: न भविष्यसि भूत्वा त्वं पुत्रपौत्रादिरूपवान् । बीजाङ्कुरवद् देहादेः व्यतिरिक्तो यथानलः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: na bhaviṣyasi bhūtvā tvaṃ putrapautrādirūpavān bījāṅkuravad dehādeḥ vyatirikto yathānalaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation: You are quite distinct from your body, etc., just as fire (though potentially abiding in fuel) is distinct from the fuel. Hence, on the analogy of seeds and trees, you i.e. your soul (being once a father[1]) shall not take the form of a son, a grandson, etc.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: The quotations from Śrutis e.g. ātmā vai putra-nāmāsi, support physical heredity and not a continuity of one’s soul—dehād deho jāyate na ātmā. Hence in this physical continuity of the race, the soul is not involved.

devanagari: स्वप्ने यथा शिरश्छेदं पञ्चत्वाद्यात्मनः स्वयम् । यस्मात्पश्यति देहस्य तत आत्मा ह्यजोऽमरः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: svapne yathā śiraśchedaṃ pañcatvādyātmanaḥ svayam yasmātpaśyati dehasya tata ātmā hyajo

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tan class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ tan class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ tan class 8 verb], [locative single from √ tan class 8 verb]
[nominative dual from √ tan class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ tan class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ tan class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ tan class 8 verb]

translation: Just as a person sees in his dream that he is beheaded (when factually he is the unaffected party or a witness to the scene of cutting the head of his body in the dream state), so also in a state of wakefulness he sees the death of his body. But as a matter of fact the Soul (ātman) is neither born nor dead.

devanagari: घटे भिन्ने घटाकाश आकाशः स्याद् यथा पुरा । एवं देहे मृते जीवो ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते पुनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ghaṭe bhinne ghaṭākāśa ākāśaḥ syād yathā purā evaṃ dehe mṛte jīvo brahma sampadyate punaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [locative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

[nominative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [locative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

[nominative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation: When an earthen pot is broken, the space enclosed within the pot becomes the same as before (the creation of that pot), in the same way when (after self-realization which destroys all Karmas) the body falls off, the individual soul becomes one with Brahman).[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: Becomes a mukta—an emancipated soul. Viśiṣṭādvaita presumes the continuity of the distinct identity of the soul even in the Mokṣa stage and not its absorption and loss in Paramātman.

devanagari: मनः सृजति वै देहान् गुणान् कर्माणि चात्मनः । तन्मनः सृजते माया ततो जीवस्य संसृतिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: manaḥ sṛjati vai dehān guṇān karmāṇi cātmanaḥ tanmanaḥ sṛjate māyā tato jīvasya saṃsṛtiḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: It is the Māyā that creates the mind[3]. The mind creates these bodies, objects of senses and actions of the soul. Hence it is due to the Māyā that the jīva (individual soul) is involved in transmigration of saṃsāra.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī: Māyā is the unintelligent (jaḍa) matter. So its creation—the mind—is also Jaḍa. The use of the Ātmanepaḍa sṛjate in tanmanaḥ sṛjate māyā, in the text of this verse indicates that it is due to the mind’s contact with the intelligent being that activates it and enables it do all these (Sṛjata ityātmane- pada-prayogeṇa cin-miśram mana iti sūcayati).

devanagari: स्नेहाधिष्ठानवर्त्यग्नि संयोगो यावदीयते । ततो दीपस्य दीपत्वं एवं देहकृतो भवः । रजःसत्त्वतमोवृत्त्या जायतेऽथ विनश्यति ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: snehādhiṣṭhānavartyagni saṃyogo yāvadīyate tato dīpasya dīpatvaṃ evaṃ dehakṛto bhavaḥ rajaḥsattvatamovṛttyā jāyate
translation: Just as the light (of an oil-lamp) retains its characteristic of giving light so long as the fire (the flame of the lamp) is associated with the wick soaked in oil, supply of oil to the wick, a container—a storage for this oil supply, similarly, Karma is the oil, the mind, the container of the oil, the body, the wick and its contact with fire means relation to the spirit. The body comes into existence and dies through the actions of the guṇas rajas, Sattva and tamas.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
The saṃsāra continues as long as the contact or influence of guṇas is there and the false presumption of the identity of the body and the soul persists. The saṃsāra like the lamp may cease to exist but the soul persists in existence despite the non-recurrcnce of the body.

devanagari: न तत्रात्मा स्वयंज्योतिः यो व्यक्ताव्यक्तयोः परः । आकाश इव चाधारो ध्रुवोऽनन्तोपमस्ततः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: na tatrātmā svayaṃjyotiḥ yo vyaktāvyaktayoḥ paraḥ ākāśa iva cādhāro dhruvo

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: The soul that is embodied does not cease to exist. For He is self-illuminating, distinct from and beyond gross (physical) and subtle, unmanifest (astral) bodies. Like the sky, he is the support (of everything else), eternal, endless, unparalleled and all pervasive.
Fully absorbed in the meditation on Vāsudeva and by means of your logical reasoning, do you yourself ponder over the self that is abiding in your body, O great king.

devanagari: चोदितो विप्रवाक्येन न त्वां धक्ष्यति तक्षकः । मृत्यवो नोपधक्ष्यन्ति मृत्यूनां मृत्युमीश्वरम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: codito vipravākyena na tvāṃ dhakṣyati takṣakaḥ mṛtyavo nopadhakṣyanti mṛtyūnāṃ mṛtyumīśvaram

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [instrumental single from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[instrumental single from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: Impelled by the imprecation of that Brāhmaṇa, Takṣaka shall not be able to burn you[5] (i.e. your soul). All the causes of death can never burn the supreme Lord who himself is the death of such agents of death.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: You will not feel the snake-bite as the causes of death cannot affect God and you who are absorbed in his contemplation
Padaratnāvalī: Due to contemplation of God, Parīkṣit would be in the proximity of God.

devanagari: अहं ब्रह्म परं धाम ब्रह्माहं परमं पदम् । एवं समीक्ष्य चात्मानम् आत्मन्याधाय निष्कले ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ brahma paraṃ dhāma brahmāhaṃ paramaṃ padam evaṃ samīkṣya cātmānam ātmanyādhāya niṣkale

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: “I am the Brahman. I am the Supreme abode. Brahman, the highest goal (to be reached) is I myself.” Having realized this and absorbing yourself in the Supreme Self which is beyond the limits or conditions, you will see neither Takṣaka who is biting you at the foot with poisonous fangs (or licking it with his poisonous mouth) nor your body nor the world as being distinct and separate from your own Ātman.

devanagari: दशन्तं तक्षकं पादे लेलिहानं विषाननैः । न द्रक्ष्यसि शरीरं च विश्वं च पृथगात्मनः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: daśantaṃ takṣakaṃ pāde lelihānaṃ viṣānanaiḥ na drakṣyasi śarīraṃ ca viśvaṃ ca pṛthagātmanaḥ
translation: “I am the Brahman. I am the Supreme abode. Brahman, the highest goal (to be reached) is I myself.” Having realized this and absorbing yourself in the Supreme Self which is beyond the limits or conditions, you will see neither Takṣaka who is biting you at the foot with poisonous fangs (or licking it with his poisonous mouth) nor your body nor the world as being distinct and separate from your own Ātman.

devanagari: एतत्ते कथितं तात यदात्मा पृष्टवान् नृप । हरेर्विश्वात्मनश्चेष्टां किं भूयः श्रोतुमिच्छसि ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: etatte kathitaṃ tāta yadātmā pṛṣṭavān nṛpa harerviśvātmanaśceṣṭāṃ kiṃ bhūyaḥ śrotumicchasi

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ kath class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ kath class 10 verb]

translation:
Dear Parīkṣit I All this has been elucidated to you as per enquiries made by you about the sportful activities of Lord Hari, the Soul of the Universe. What more do you wish to hear from me, O king!
